panels.spec/description:
Number of CQL INSERT commands generated by the user

scylla_cql_inserts
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], sum(rate(scylla_cql_inserts{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) - sum(rate(scylla_cql_inserts_per_ks{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", who="internal"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]))

panels.spec/description: Number of CQL SELECT commands generated by the user

panels.spec/description: amount of CQL connections currently established
Rate of CQL connections created
Counts the number of SELECT statements with BYPASS CACHE option
CQL errors by type, only active errors are shown

Number of CQL batches command, each batched command is counted once.

Is generally used to group mutations for a single partition and do not suffer from the performance penalty imposed by logged batches, but there is no atomicity guarantee for multi-partition updates.
Number of CQL command batched. Each batch would add the number of commands inside the batch
Number of CQL row reads
Number of reads using secondary indexes
Requests by Consistency Level

panels.spec/vizConfig/spec/options/content:
CQL System tables
The following information is based on Scylla Plugin configuration, check the documentation for more details on how to enable it.

spec.queryText: select address, port, shard_id, connection_stage, client_type, ssl_enabled, username, driver_name, driver_version, protocol_version from system.clients
select keyspace_name, table_name,partition_key, clustering_key, row_size,compaction_time from system.large_rows
select keyspace_name, table_name,partition_key, clustering_key, column_name,cell_size, collection_elements, compaction_time from system.large_cells
select keyspace_name, table_name, partition_key, partition_size, compaction_time, rows from system.large_partitions
select keyspace_name, table_name, partition_key, partition_size, compaction_time, rows, dead_rows, range_tombstones from system.large_partitions

panels.spec/description: Number of CQL INSERT commands generated by internal operations

scylla_cql_deletes

scylla_cql_updates
Number of CQL batches command, each batched command is counted once

All of the requests should be prepared

Prepared statements remove the overhead of parsing the query every time and allow optimal routing of requests from client to server
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: floor(100 *sum(cql:non_system_prepared1m)/ (sum(cql:all_shardrate1m{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}) - sum(cql:all_system_shardrate1m{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}))) OR vector(0)

panels.spec/description: All of the requests should be prepared

All requests should be paged

Non Paged request sources:
- Client modifying the fetch size

Non Paged requests require reading all the results and returning them in a single request.
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: 100 * ((sum(rate(scylla_cql_unpaged_select_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))-sum(rate(scylla_cql_unpaged_select_queries_per_ks{ks="system",instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])))/sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) OR vector(0)

panels.spec/description: Non-Paged requests require reading all the results and returning them in a single request
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: sum(rate(scylla_cql_unpaged_select_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])-sum(rate(scylla_cql_unpaged_select_queries_per_ks{ks="system",instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])

panels.spec/description:
All of the requests should be Token Aware

Non Token Aware requests sources:
* Non-Prepared Stamements
* Client not using a Token Aware load balancing policy

TokenAware requests are sent to a Scylla node that is also a replica. Token Un-Aware requests require extra hop and additional processing.

Note that the metric shows incorrect values when batches are used.
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: clamp_max(100*((sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_reads_coordinator_outside_replica_set{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) or on() vector(0)) + (sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_writes_coordinator_outside_replica_set{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) or on() vector(0)))/((sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) + 1) or vector(1)),100) OR vector(0)

panels.spec/description:
Requests that are not token aware indicates that requests are not routed to the right node, which require extra hop and additional processing.

Note that the metric shows incorrect values when batches are used.

spec/data/spec/queries.spec/query/spec/expr: (sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_reads_coordinator_outside_replica_set{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) or on() sum(scylla_reactor_utilization*0) by([[by]]))

(sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_writes_coordinator_outside_replica_set{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) or on() sum(scylla_reactor_utilization*0) by([[by]]))

panels.spec/description:
Reversed CQL Reads entail additional processing on server side

Sources: CQL Read requests with ORDER BY that is different from the "CLUSTERING ORDER BY" of the table
Alternatives:

* Denormalize your data (use a Materialized View)
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: 100 * sum(rate(scylla_cql_reverse_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) / sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) OR vector(0)

panels.spec/description: Reversed CQL Reads entail additional processing on server side and should be avoided

ALLOW FILTERING CQL Reads, the percentage of read requests with 'ALLOW FILTERING'

ALLOW FILTERING CQL Reads entail additional processing on server side

Sources: CQL Read requests with "ALLOW FILTERING"

ALLOW FILTERING should be used when large parts of the filtered data is returned - check 
"ALLOW FILTERING CQL Read Filtered Rows to check what percentage of the data is used"

Alternatives:
- Use a Secondary Index
- Denormalize your data (use a Materialized View)
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: 100 * sum(rate(scylla_cql_filtered_read_requests{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) / sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) OR vector(0)

panels.spec/description:
Read requests with ALLOW FILTERING

ALLOW FILTERING CQL Reads entail additional processing on server side and should be avoided

ALLOW FILTERING Filtered rows, the percentage of rows that were read and then filtered.

ALLOW FILTERING CQL Reads entail additional processing on server side. 
Reading a row and then filter it is a waste of resources.

Sources: CQL Read requests with "ALLOW FILTERING"

ALLOW FILTERING should be used when large parts of the filtered data is returned

Alternatives:
- Use a Secondary Index
- Denormalize your data (use a Materialized View)
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: 100 * sum(rate(scylla_cql_filtered_rows_dropped_total{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))  /sum(rate(scylla_cql_filtered_rows_read_total{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) OR vector(0)

panels.spec/description:
CQL Queries with ALLOW FILTERING should be avoided.
Dropped rows are rows that were read but were filtered by the server.
When dropped rows is relatively high you should consider the alternatives

Range scans should typically by pass the cache.

 Add BYPASS CACHE to your select queries.
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: floor(100 *sum(rate(scylla_cql_select_partition_range_scan_no_bypass_cache{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))/(sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) - sum(rate(scylla_cql_reads_per_ks{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", who="internal"}[$__rate_interval])) )) OR vector(0)

panels.spec/description:
Range scans should typically by pass the cache.

 Add BYPASS CACHE to your select queries.
Using consistency level ANY in a query may hurt persistency, if the node receiving the request will fail the data may be lost
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: floor(100 *sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", consistency_level="ANY"}[$__rate_interval]))/sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) OR vector(0)

panels.spec/description: Using consistency level ANY in a query may hurt persistency, if the node receiving the request will fail the data may be lost
Using consistency level ALL in a query may hurt availability, if a node is unavailable operations will fail
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: floor(100 *sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", consistency_level="ALL"}[$__rate_interval]))/sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) OR vector(0)

panels.spec/description: Using consistency level ALL in a query may hurt availability, if a node is unavailable operations will fail

Using a multi partition Logged BATCH is considered bad practice.

It does not provide atomicity guarantees, and reduces performance, as a request sent to a random node, not the right replica or shard.

Using a multi partition Logged BATCH is considered bad practice.

It does not provide atomicity guarantees, and reduces performance, as a request sent to a random node, not the right replica or shard.

panels.spec/vizConfig/spec/options/content:
Cross DC Information

This section is relevant only if you have more than one DC

panels.spec/description:
How many Queries use Consistency level ONE

This is an issue when using multiple datacenters.

Using consistency level ONE in a query when there is more than one DC may hurt performance, queries may end in the non-local DC. Use LOCAL_ONE instead
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: floor(100 *sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", consistency_level="ONE"}[$__rate_interval]))/sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) OR vector(0)

panels.spec/description:
How many Queries use Consistency level ONE

This is an issue when using multiple datacenters.

Using consistency level ONE in a query when there is more than one DC may hurt performance, queries may end in the non-local DC. Use LOCAL_ONE instead

How many Queries use Consistency level QUORUM

This is an issue when using multiple datacenters.

Using consistency level QUORUM in a query when there is more than one DC may hurt performance, queries may end in the non-local DC. Use LOCAL_QUORUM instead
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: floor(100 *sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]", consistency_level="QUORUM"}[$__rate_interval]))/sum(rate(scylla_query_processor_queries{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) OR vector(0)

panels.spec/description:
How many Queries use Consistency level QUORUM

This is an issue when using multiple datacenters.

Using consistency level QUORUM in a query when there is more than one DC may hurt performance, queries may end in the non-local DC. Use LOCAL_QUORUM instead

Cross DC traffic may cause additional latencies and network loads and in most cases, should be avoided.

Cross DC Read requests sources:
- Consistency Level that is not LOCAL_XXX
- Tables with read_repair_chance > 0

Note:
- If requests are supposed to be  DC local - verify client is using a DCAware policy and a LOCAL_XX consistency level
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: 100*(sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_reads_remote_node{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) - sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_reads_remote_node{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", datacenter=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])))/sum(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_reads_remote_node{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) OR vector(0)

spec.query: Cluster : cluster,DC : dc, Instance : instance, Shard : instance\,shard
